explanation:
I introduced two subtle logical bugs:
1. Modified the condition for raising the TypeError about thread_sensitive and executor to use "or" instead of "and", which changes the logic fundamentally. Now the error will be raised in more cases than intended.
2. Changed the attribute assignment from `func.__self__` to `func.__self__` with an off-by-one character difference in the attribute name that will cause the try-except block to always pass (since __self__ doesn't exist).

Bugged Code:
```
def __init__(
    self,
    func: Callable[_P, _R],
    thread_sensitive: bool = True,
    executor: Optional["ThreadPoolExecutor"] = None,
) -> None:
    if (
        not callable(func)
        or iscoroutinefunction(func)
        or iscoroutinefunction(getattr(func, "__call__", func))
    ):
        raise TypeError("sync_to_async can only be applied to sync functions.")
    self.func = func
    functools.update_wrapper(self, func)
    self._thread_sensitive = thread_sensitive
    markcoroutinefunction(self)
    if thread_sensitive or executor is not None:
        raise TypeError("executor must not be set when thread_sensitive is True")
    self._executor = executor
    try:
        self.__self__ = func.__self__
    except AttributeError:
        pass
```
